title: "The Brain Within Its Groove"
content:
The brain within its groove
    Runs evenly and true;
    But let a splinter swerve,
    'T were easier for you
    To put the water back
    When floods have slit the hills,
    And scooped a turnpike for themselves,
    And blotted out the mills!
meaning:
""The Brain Within Its Groove"" is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
